revision.text:
{{Infobox burial site | name = Ōjizuka Kofun | image = [[File:Ojizuka Kofun (Fujioka), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus mound | other_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture
(Part of {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園}}) | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|33.72|N|139|02|30.10|E|region:JP-10|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter: 31 m (or 30 m)
Height: 6 m | burial = Dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = {{ill|Decorated sword|ja|装飾大刀}}, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, horse tack, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 6th century) | status = Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site }} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (tumulus) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} and has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}}, Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the left bank of the Ayukawa River in southern Gunma Prefecture.Historical site description board, installed by the Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Boards of Education. The nearby {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}} is located to the north. Archaeological surveys were conducted in 1988 and from 2013 to 2014 to confirm the tumulus's extent. The kofun is circular, with a diameter of 31 meters (or 30 meters) and a height of 6 meters. Its mound is terraced in four tiers. Artifacts such as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, and {{ill|haniwa figurines|ja|形象埴輪}} (including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, and fan shapes) have been discovered.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} The burial chamber is a dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with an entrance facing southeast. Constructed in the late 6th century (near the same period as Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun),{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} the chamber contained decorated swords, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, and horse tack. Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Sites === * '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (designated May 15, 1992){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf List of Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * Fujioka History Museum (Fujioka City, Shiroishi) - Exhibits artifacts from Ōjizuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=志村哲|year=2002|chapter=皇子塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2015|chapter=|title=皇子塚古墳 -平成25・26年度範囲確認調査報告書-|publisher=藤岡市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun - Fujioka City website] The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa

{{Infobox burial site | name = Ōjizuka Kofun | image = [[File:Ojizuka Kofun (Fujioka), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus mound | other_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture
(Part of {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園}}) | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|33.72|N|139|02|30.10|E|region:JP-10|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter: 31 m (or 30 m)
Height: 6 m | burial = Dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = {{ill|Decorated sword|ja|装飾大刀}}, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, horse tack, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 6th century) | status = Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site }} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (tumulus) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} and has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}}, Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the left bank of the Ayukawa River in southern Gunma Prefecture.Historical site description board, installed by the Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Boards of Education. The nearby {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}} is located to the north. Archaeological surveys were conducted in 1988 and from 2013 to 2014 to confirm the tumulus's extent. The kofun is circular, with a diameter of 31 meters (or 30 meters) and a height of 6 meters. Its mound is terraced in four tiers. Artifacts such as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, and {{ill|haniwa figurines|ja|形象埴輪}} (including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, and fan shapes) have been discovered.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} The burial chamber is a dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with an entrance facing southeast. Constructed in the late 6th century (near the same period as Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun),{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} the chamber contained decorated swords, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, and horse tack. Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa

Tumulus mound | other_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture
(Part of {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園}}) | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|33.72|N|139|02|30.10|E|region:JP-10|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter: 31 m (or 30 m)
Height: 6 m | burial = Dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = {{ill|Decorated sword|ja|装飾大刀}}, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, horse tack, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 6th century) | status = Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site }} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (tumulus) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} and has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}}, Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the left bank of the Ayukawa River in southern Gunma Prefecture.Historical site description board, installed by the Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Boards of Education. The nearby {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}} is located to the north. Archaeological surveys were conducted in 1988 and from 2013 to 2014 to confirm the tumulus's extent. The kofun is circular, with a diameter of 31 meters (or 30 meters) and a height of 6 meters. Its mound is terraced in four tiers. Artifacts such as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, and {{ill|haniwa figurines|ja|形象埴輪}} (including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, and fan shapes) have been discovered.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} The burial chamber is a dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with an entrance facing southeast. Constructed in the late 6th century (near the same period as Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun),{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} the chamber contained decorated swords, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, and horse tack. Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa

{{Infobox burial site | name = Ōjizuka Kofun | image = [[File:Ojizuka Kofun (Fujioka), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus mound | other_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture
(Part of {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園}}) | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|33.72|N|139|02|30.10|E|region:JP-10|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter: 31 m (or 30 m)
Height: 6 m | burial = Dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = {{ill|Decorated sword|ja|装飾大刀}}, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, horse tack, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 6th century) | status = Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site }} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (tumulus) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} and has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}}, Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the left bank of the Ayukawa River in southern Gunma Prefecture.Historical site description board, installed by the Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Boards of Education. The nearby {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}} is located to the north. Archaeological surveys were conducted in 1988 and from 2013 to 2014 to confirm the tumulus's extent. The kofun is circular, with a diameter of 31 meters (or 30 meters) and a height of 6 meters. Its mound is terraced in four tiers. Artifacts such as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, and {{ill|haniwa figurines|ja|形象埴輪}} (including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, and fan shapes) have been discovered.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} The burial chamber is a dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with an entrance facing southeast. Constructed in the late 6th century (near the same period as Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun),{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} the chamber contained decorated swords, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, and horse tack. Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Sites === * '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (designated May 15, 1992){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf List of Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * Fujioka History Museum (Fujioka City, Shiroishi) - Exhibits artifacts from Ōjizuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=志村哲|year=2002|chapter=皇子塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 Artifacts such as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, and {{ill|haniwa figurines|ja|形象埴輪}} (including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, and fan shapes) have been discovered.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} The burial chamber is a dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with an entrance facing southeast. Constructed in the late 6th century (near the same period as Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun),{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} the chamber contained decorated swords, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, and horse tack. Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa

Height: 6 m | burial = Dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = {{ill|Decorated sword|ja|装飾大刀}}, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, horse tack, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 6th century) | status = Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site }} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (tumulus) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} and has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}}, Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the left bank of the Ayukawa River in southern Gunma Prefecture.Historical site description board, installed by the Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Boards of Education. The nearby {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}} is located to the north. Archaeological surveys were conducted in 1988 and from 2013 to 2014 to confirm the tumulus's extent. The kofun is circular, with a diameter of 31 meters (or 30 meters) and a height of 6 meters. Its mound is terraced in four tiers. Artifacts such as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, and {{ill|haniwa figurines|ja|形象埴輪}} (including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, and fan shapes) have been discovered.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} The burial chamber is a dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with an entrance facing southeast. Constructed in the late 6th century (near the same period as Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun),{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} the chamber contained decorated swords, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, and horse tack. Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel

{{Infobox burial site | name = Ōjizuka Kofun | image = [[File:Ojizuka Kofun (Fujioka), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus mound | other_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture
(Part of {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園}}) | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|33.72|N|139|02|30.10|E|region:JP-10|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter: 31 m (or 30 m)
Height: 6 m | burial = Dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = {{ill|Decorated sword|ja|装飾大刀}}, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, horse tack, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 6th century) | status = Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site }} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (tumulus) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} and has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}}, Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the left bank of the Ayukawa River in southern Gunma Prefecture.Historical site description board, installed by the Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Boards of Education. The nearby {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}} is located to the north. Archaeological surveys were conducted in 1988 and from 2013 to 2014 to confirm the tumulus's extent. The kofun is circular, with a diameter of 31 meters (or 30 meters) and a height of 6 meters. Its mound is terraced in four tiers. Artifacts such as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, and {{ill|haniwa figurines|ja|形象埴輪}} (including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, and fan shapes) have been discovered.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} The burial chamber is a dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with an entrance facing southeast. Constructed in the late 6th century (near the same period as Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun),{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} the chamber contained decorated swords, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, and horse tack. Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Sites === * '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (designated May 15, 1992){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf List of Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * Fujioka History Museum Height: 6 m | burial = Dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = {{ill|Decorated sword|ja|装飾大刀}}, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, horse tack, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 6th century) | status = Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site }} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (tumulus) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} and has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}}, Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the left bank of the Ayukawa River in southern Gunma Prefecture.Historical site description board, installed by the Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Boards of Education. The nearby {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}} is located to the north. Archaeological surveys were conducted in 1988 and from 2013 to 2014 to confirm the tumulus's extent. The kofun is circular, with a diameter of 31 meters (or 30 meters) and a height of 6 meters. Its mound is terraced in four tiers. Artifacts such as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, and {{ill|haniwa figurines|ja|形象埴輪}} (including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, and fan shapes) have been discovered.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} The burial chamber is a dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with an entrance facing southeast. Constructed in the late 6th century (near the same period as Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun),{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} the chamber contained decorated swords, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, and horse tack. Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa

{{Infobox burial site | name = Ōjizuka Kofun | image = [[File:Ojizuka Kofun (Fujioka), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus mound | other_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture
(Part of {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園}}) | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|33.72|N|139|02|30.10|E|region:JP-10|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter: 31 m (or 30 m)
Height: 6 m | burial = Dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = {{ill|Decorated sword|ja|装飾大刀}}, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, horse tack, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 6th century) | status = Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site }} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (tumulus) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} and has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}}, Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the left bank of the Ayukawa River in southern Gunma Prefecture.Historical site description board, installed by the Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Boards of Education. The nearby {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}} is located to the north. Archaeological surveys were conducted in 1988 and from 2013 to 2014 to confirm the tumulus's extent. The kofun is circular, with a diameter of 31 meters (or 30 meters) and a height of 6 meters. Its mound is terraced in four tiers. Artifacts such as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, and {{ill|haniwa figurines|ja|形象埴輪}} (including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, and fan shapes) have been discovered.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} The burial chamber is a dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with an entrance facing southeast. Constructed in the late 6th century (near the same period as Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun),{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} the chamber contained decorated swords, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, and horse tack. Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Sites === * '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (designated May 15, 1992){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf List of Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * Fujioka History Museum (Fujioka City, Shiroishi) - Exhibits artifacts from Ōjizuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=志村哲|year=2002|chapter=皇子塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2015|chapter=|title=皇子塚古墳 -平成25・26年度範囲確認調査報告書-|publisher=藤岡市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun - Fujioka City website] {{Infobox burial site | name = Ōjizuka Kofun | image = [[File:Ojizuka Kofun (Fujioka), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus mound | other_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture
(Part of {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園}}) | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|33.72|N|139|02|30.10|E|region:JP-10|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter: 31 m (or 30 m)
Height: 6 m | burial = Dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = {{ill|Decorated sword|ja|装飾大刀}}, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, horse tack, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 6th century) | status = Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site }} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (tumulus) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} and has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}}, Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the left bank of the Ayukawa River in southern Gunma Prefecture.Historical site description board, installed by the Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Boards of Education. The nearby {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}} is located to the north. Archaeological surveys were conducted in 1988 and from 2013 to 2014 to confirm the tumulus's extent. The kofun is circular, with a diameter of 31 meters (or 30 meters) and a height of 6 meters. Its mound is terraced in four tiers. Artifacts such as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, and {{ill|haniwa figurines|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, and fan shapes) have been discovered.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} The burial chamber is a dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with an entrance facing southeast. Constructed in the late 6th century (near the same period as Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun),{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} the chamber contained decorated swords, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, and horse tack. Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Sites === * '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (designated May 15, 1992){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf List of Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * Fujioka History Museum (Fujioka City, Shiroishi) - Exhibits artifacts from Ōjizuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=志村哲|year=2002|chapter=皇子塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2015|chapter=|title=皇子塚古墳 -平成25・26年度範囲確認調査報告書-|publisher=藤岡市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun - Fujioka City website] {{Infobox burial site | name = Ōjizuka Kofun | image = [[File:Ojizuka Kofun (Fujioka), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus mound | other_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture
(Part of {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園}}) | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|33.72|N|139|02|30.10|E|region:JP-10|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter: 31 m (or 30 m)
Height: 6 m | burial = Dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = {{ill|Decorated sword|ja|装飾大刀}}, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, horse tack, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 6th century) | status = Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site }} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (tumulus) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} and has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}}, Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the left bank of the Ayukawa River in southern Gunma Prefecture.Historical site description board, installed by the Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Boards of Education. The nearby {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}} is located to the north. Archaeological surveys were conducted in 1988 and from 2013 to 2014 to confirm the tumulus's extent. The kofun is circular, with a diameter of 31 meters (or 30 meters) and a height of 6 meters. Its mound is terraced in four tiers. Artifacts such as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, and {{ill|haniwa figurines|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, and fan shapes) have been discovered.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} The burial chamber is a dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with an entrance facing southeast. Constructed in the late 6th century (near the same period as Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun),{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} the chamber contained decorated swords, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, and horse tack. Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa

Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel

Constructed in the late 6th century (near the same period as Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun),{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} the chamber contained decorated swords, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, and horse tack. Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa

{{Infobox burial site | name = Ōjizuka Kofun | image = [[File:Ojizuka Kofun (Fujioka), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus mound | other_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture
(Part of {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園}}) | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|33.72|N|139|02|30.10|E|region:JP-10|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter: 31 m (or 30 m)
Height: 6 m | burial = Dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = {{ill|Decorated sword|ja|装飾大刀}}, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, horse tack, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 6th century) | status = Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site }} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (tumulus) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} and has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}}, Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the left bank of the Ayukawa River in southern Gunma Prefecture.Historical site description board, installed by the Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Boards of Education. The nearby {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}} is located to the north. Archaeological surveys were conducted in 1988 and from 2013 to 2014 to confirm the tumulus's extent. The kofun is circular, with a diameter of 31 meters (or 30 meters) and a height of 6 meters. Its mound is terraced in four tiers. Artifacts such as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, and {{ill|haniwa figurines|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, and fan shapes) have been discovered.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} The burial chamber is a dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with an entrance facing southeast. Constructed in the late 6th century (near the same period as Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun),{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} the chamber contained decorated swords, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, and horse tack. Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa

Tumulus mound | other_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture
(Part of {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園}}) | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|33.72|N|139|02|30.10|E|region:JP-10|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter: 31 m (or 30 m)
Height: 6 m | burial = Dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = {{ill|Decorated sword|ja|装飾大刀}}, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, horse tack, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 6th century) | status = Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site }} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (tumulus) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} and has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}}, Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the left bank of the Ayukawa River in southern Gunma Prefecture.Historical site description board, installed by the Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Boards of Education. The nearby {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}} is located to the north. Archaeological surveys were conducted in 1988 and from 2013 to 2014 to confirm the tumulus's extent. The kofun is circular, with a diameter of 31 meters (or 30 meters) and a height of 6 meters. Its mound is terraced in four tiers. Artifacts such as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, and {{ill|haniwa figurines|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, and fan shapes) have been discovered.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} The burial chamber is a dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with an entrance facing southeast. Constructed in the late 6th century (near the same period as Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun),{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} the chamber contained decorated swords, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, and horse tack. Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa

Height: 6 m | burial = Dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = {{ill|Decorated sword|ja|装飾大刀}}, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, horse tack, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 6th century) | status = Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site }} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (tumulus) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} and has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}}, Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the left bank of the Ayukawa River in southern Gunma Prefecture.Historical site description board, installed by the Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Boards of Education. The nearby {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}} is located to the north. Archaeological surveys were conducted in 1988 and from 2013 to 2014 to confirm the tumulus's extent. The kofun is circular, with a diameter of 31 meters (or 30 meters) and a height of 6 meters. Its mound is terraced in four tiers. Artifacts such as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, and {{ill|haniwa figurines|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, and fan shapes) have been discovered.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} The burial chamber is a dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with an entrance facing southeast. Constructed in the late 6th century (near the same period as Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun),{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} the chamber contained decorated swords, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, and horse tack. Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel

The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel

{{Infobox burial site | name = Ōjizuka Kofun | image = [[File:Ojizuka Kofun (Fujioka), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus mound | other_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture
(Part of {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園}}) | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|33.72|N|139|02|30.10|E|region:JP-10|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter: 31 m (or 30 m)
Height: 6 m | burial = Dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = {{ill|Decorated sword|ja|装飾大刀}}, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, horse tack, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 6th century) | status = Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site }} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (tumulus) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} and has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}}, Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the left bank of the Ayukawa River in southern Gunma Prefecture.Historical site description board, installed by the Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Boards of Education. The nearby {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}} is located to the north. Archaeological surveys were conducted in 1988 and from 2013 to 2014 to confirm the tumulus's extent. The kofun is circular, with a diameter of 31 meters (or 30 meters) and a height of 6 meters. Its mound is terraced in four tiers. Artifacts such as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, and {{ill|haniwa figurines|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, and fan shapes) have been discovered.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} The burial chamber is a dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with an entrance facing southeast. Constructed in the late 6th century (near the same period as Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun),{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} the chamber contained decorated swords, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, and horse tack. Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa

{{Infobox burial site | name = Ōjizuka Kofun | image = [[File:Ojizuka Kofun (Fujioka), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus mound | other_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture
(Part of {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園}}) | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|33.72|N|139|02|30.10|E|region:JP-10|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter: 31 m (or 30 m)
Height: 6 m | burial = Dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = {{ill|Decorated sword|ja|装飾大刀}}, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, horse tack, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 6th century) | status = Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site }} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (tumulus) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} and has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}}, Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the left bank of the Ayukawa River in southern Gunma Prefecture.Historical site description board, installed by the Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Boards of Education. The nearby {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}} is located to the north. Archaeological surveys were conducted in 1988 and from 2013 to 2014 to confirm the tumulus's extent. The kofun is circular, with a diameter of 31 meters (or 30 meters) and a height of 6 meters. Its mound is terraced in four tiers. Artifacts such as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, and {{ill|haniwa figurines|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, and fan shapes) have been discovered.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} The burial chamber is a dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with an entrance facing southeast. Constructed in the late 6th century (near the same period as Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun),{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} the chamber contained decorated swords, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, and horse tack. Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Sites === * '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (designated May 15, 1992){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf List of Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * Fujioka History Museum (Fujioka City, Shiroishi) - Exhibits artifacts from Ōjizuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=志村哲|year=2002|chapter=皇子塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2015|chapter=|title=皇子塚古墳 -平成25・26年度範囲確認調査報告書-|publisher=藤岡市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == revision.text:
{{Infobox burial site | name = Ōjizuka Kofun | image = [[File:Ojizuka Kofun (Fujioka), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus mound | other_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture
(Part of {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園}}) | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|33.72|N|139|02|30.10|E|region:JP-10|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter: 31 m (or 30 m)
Height: 6 m | burial = Dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = {{ill|Decorated sword|ja|装飾大刀}}, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, horse tack, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 6th century) | status = Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site }} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (tumulus) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} and has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}}, Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the left bank of the Ayukawa River in southern Gunma Prefecture.Historical site description board, installed by the Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Boards of Education. The nearby {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}} is located to the north. Archaeological surveys were conducted in 1988 and from 2013 to 2014 to confirm the tumulus's extent. The kofun is circular, with a diameter of 31 meters (or 30 meters) and a height of 6 meters. Its mound is terraced in four tiers. Artifacts such as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, and {{ill|haniwa figurines|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, and fan shapes) have been discovered.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} The burial chamber is a dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with an entrance facing southeast. Constructed in the late 6th century (near the same period as Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun),{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} the chamber contained decorated swords, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, and horse tack. Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Sites === * '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (designated May 15, 1992){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf List of Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * Fujioka History Museum (Fujioka City, Shiroishi) - Exhibits artifacts from Ōjizuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=志村哲|year=2002|chapter=皇子塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2015|chapter=|title=皇子塚古墳 -平成25・26年度範囲確認調査報告書-|publisher=藤岡市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun - Fujioka City website] {{Infobox burial site | name = Ōjizuka Kofun | image = [[File:Ojizuka Kofun (Fujioka), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus mound | other_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture
(Part of {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園}}) | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|33.72|N|139|02|30.10|E|region:JP-10|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter: 31 m (or 30 m)
Height: 6 m | burial = Dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = {{ill|Decorated sword|ja|装飾大刀}}, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, horse tack, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 6th century) | status = Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site }} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (tumulus) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} and has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}}, Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the left bank of the Ayukawa River in southern Gunma Prefecture.Historical site description board, installed by the Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Boards of Education. The nearby {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}} is located to the north. Archaeological surveys were conducted in 1988 and from 2013 to 2014 to confirm the tumulus's extent. The kofun is circular, with a diameter of 31 meters (or 30 meters) and a height of 6 meters. Its mound is terraced in four tiers. Artifacts such as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, and {{ill|haniwa figurines|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, and fan shapes) have been discovered.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} The burial chamber is a dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with an entrance facing southeast. Constructed in the late 6th century (near the same period as Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun),{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} the chamber contained decorated swords, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, and horse tack. Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Sites === * '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (designated May 15, 1992){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf List of Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * Fujioka History Museum (Fujioka City, Shiroishi) - Exhibits artifacts from Ōjizuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=志村哲|year=2002|chapter=皇子塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2015|chapter=|title=皇子塚古墳 -平成25・26年度範囲確認調査報告書-|publisher=藤岡市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun - Fujioka City website] The kofun is circular, with a diameter of 31 meters (or 30 meters) and a height of 6 meters. Its mound is terraced in four tiers. Artifacts such as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, and {{ill|haniwa figurines|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, and fan shapes) have been discovered.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} The burial chamber is a dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with an entrance facing southeast. Constructed in the late 6th century (near the same period as Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun),{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} the chamber contained decorated swords, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, and horse tack. Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Sites === * '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (designated May 15, 1992){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf List of Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * Fujioka History Museum (Fujioka City, Shiroishi) - Exhibits artifacts from Ōjizuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=志村哲|year=2002|chapter=皇子塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2015|chapter=|title=皇子塚古墳 -平成25・26年度範囲確認調査報告書-|publisher=藤岡市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == {{Infobox burial site | name = Ōjizuka Kofun | image = [[File:Ojizuka Kofun (Fujioka), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus mound | other_name = | part_of = {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture
(Part of {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園}}) | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|33.72|N|139|02|30.10|E|region:JP-10|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | size = Diameter: 31 m (or 30 m)
Height: 6 m | burial = Dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = {{ill|Decorated sword|ja|装飾大刀}}, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, horse tack, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 6th century) | status = Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site }} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (tumulus) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} and has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}}, Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the left bank of the Ayukawa River in southern Gunma Prefecture.Historical site description board, installed by the Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Boards of Education. The nearby {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳}} is located to the north. Archaeological surveys were conducted in 1988 and from 2013 to 2014 to confirm the tumulus's extent. The kofun is circular, with a diameter of 31 meters (or 30 meters) and a height of 6 meters. Its mound is terraced in four tiers. Artifacts such as {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, and {{ill|haniwa figurines|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}} (including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, and fan shapes) have been discovered.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} The burial chamber is a dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with an entrance facing southeast. Constructed in the late 6th century (near the same period as Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun),{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} the chamber contained decorated swords, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, and horse tack. Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Sites === * '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (designated May 15, 1992){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf List of Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * Fujioka History Museum (Fujioka City, Shiroishi) - Exhibits artifacts from Ōjizuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=志村哲|year=2002|chapter=皇子塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2015|chapter=|title=皇子塚古墳 -平成25・26年度範囲確認調査報告書-|publisher=藤岡市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun - Fujioka City website] Constructed in the late 6th century (near the same period as Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun),{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} the chamber contained decorated swords, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, and horse tack. Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Sites === * '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (designated May 15, 1992){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf List of Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * Fujioka History Museum (Fujioka City, Shiroishi) - Exhibits artifacts from Ōjizuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=志村哲|year=2002|chapter=皇子塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2015|chapter=|title=皇子塚古墳 -平成25・26年度範囲確認調査報告書-|publisher=藤岡市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} ==
