Height: 6 m | burial = Dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | artifacts = {{ill|Decorated sword|ja|装飾大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decorated sword|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 装飾付大刀|1=decorated swords|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48749737}}, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, horse tack, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} | period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} (late 6th century) | status = Designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site }} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} (tumulus) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en|Fujioka, Gunma|WD=Q596486|lt=Fujioka, Gunma|1=Fujioka, Gunma}}, Gunma Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroishi Kofun Group|qq=}} and has been designated as a Gunma Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|qq=}}, Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the left bank of the Ayukawa River in southern Gunma Prefecture.Historical site description board, installed by the Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Boards of Education. The nearby {{ill|Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|ja|平井地区1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|qq=}} is located to the north. Archaeological surveys were conducted in 1988 and from 2013 to 2014 to confirm the tumulus's extent. The kofun is circular, with a diameter of 31 meters (or 30 meters) and a height of 6 meters. Its mound is terraced in four tiers. Artifacts such as {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}}, {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, and {{ill|haniwa figurines|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa figurines|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}} (including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, and fan shapes) have been discovered.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} The burial chamber is a dual-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with an entrance facing southeast. Constructed in the late 6th century (near the same period as Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun),{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} the chamber contained decorated swords, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, and horse tack. Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Sites === * '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (designated May 15, 1992){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf List of Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * Fujioka History Museum (Fujioka City, Shiroishi) - Exhibits artifacts from Ōjizuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=志村哲|year=2002|chapter=皇子塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2015|chapter=|title=皇子塚古墳 -平成25・26年度範囲確認調査報告書-|publisher=藤岡市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroishi Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun - Fujioka City website] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)|version=99995685|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:sn_crud|TESTŌji-zuka Kofun (Fujioka)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTE7%9A%87%E5%AD%90%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E8%97%A4%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:皇子塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Ōjizuka Kofun}} [[ja:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q106995417}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Constructed in the late 6th century (near the same period as Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun),{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} the chamber contained decorated swords, bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, small beads, and horse tack. Secondary burials occurred into the early 7th century. The site is part of the Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park and has been preserved for public access. == Burial Structure == The burial chamber is a dual-chambered yokoana-style stone chamber with the following dimensions: * Total length: 8.33 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.52 meters, width 2.1 meters, height 1.6 meters * Ante-chamber: Length 1.89 meters * Passageway: Length 2.64 meters The main chamber is constructed of neatly cut tuff blocks, while the ante-chamber and passageway are made of river stones.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Inside the chamber, tuff partition stones divide the main chamber, and an additional tuff stone is placed at the chamber entrance.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} Artifacts found in the chamber include {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} vessels and shield-shaped haniwa.{{Harv|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Single-dragon pommel
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Fujioka History Museum.}} == Cultural Heritage == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Historic Sites === * '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (designated May 15, 1992){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf List of Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Gunma Prefecture website). == Related Facilities == * Fujioka History Museum (Fujioka City, Shiroishi) - Exhibits artifacts from Ōjizuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=志村哲|year=2002|chapter=皇子塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2015|chapter=|title=皇子塚古墳 -平成25・26年度範囲確認調査報告書-|publisher=藤岡市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shiroishi Kofun Group|ja|白石古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroishi Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun - Fujioka City website] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)|version=99995685|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Ōjizuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q106995417|ja|皇子塚古墳|ja|皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with interwikis with duplicate languages]]
